opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Please help — I was reading an email and suddenly I couldn't understand the words, my brain felt like it short-circuited, I'm confused and I can't get my sentences out. My husband is really scared it might be a stroke.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on all of a sudden about half an hour after I'd been on the computer, and it hasn't cleared — I've never had it hit this fast or this hard before.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 30 minutes ago, coming on suddenly after roughly half an hour of computer work
symptom_narrative.duration: still going now, has not settled
symptom_narrative.character: suddenly couldn't make sense of the words on the screen, word-finding is very hard, feels confused and mentally 'switched off', with a heavy exhausted feeling in the head
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says it is frightening and the worst episode she has had
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: in my head / my thinking and speech
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: sudden onset, constant since it started
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: resting in a dark quiet room and coffee have not helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any screen use, reading, or trying to talk

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a bad viral illness some months ago that I never fully bounced back from — ongoing brain fog, poor memory and getting wiped out easily since

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular prescription medicines; nothing for memory or thinking

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: does a lot of screen/computer work; spouse is present and made the call; both are very distressed and afraid of a stroke or early dementia

objective_data_offered.value: on video she looks tired and keeps her eyes closed; she speaks slowly and pauses a lot to find words; her face looks symmetrical with no obvious drooping when she talks
spouse says her arms and grip feel normal and equal on both sides and she is not weak or numb down one side, but she is confused and cannot hold a normal conversation
spouse reports it started very suddenly about 30 minutes ago, a short while after she sat down at the computer, and has not improved
she says she suddenly could not understand the words she was reading, her brain 'short-circuited', and she feels completely wiped out
the room lights are dimmed and she is wearing noise-cancelling headphones; she tried a coffee with no effect
